description: O2TODERM LED PLUS delivers simultaneous oxygen and anion therapy alongside photo-dynamic LED light to saturate the skin with reactive oxygen species and stimulate mitochondrial activity. The device pairs four selectable LED wavelengths with pressurised oxygen infusion to support post-procedure recovery and barrier repair. It integrates with the ULDM ultrasound unit, enabling operators to combine photodynamic and ultrasonic modalities within a single coordinated clinic protocol.
protocol: Select the LED wavelength appropriate to the indication (e.g. 415 nm for acne, 630 nm for rejuvenation, 830 nm for recovery), set oxygen flow pressure per the device manual, and position the applicator 5–10 cm from the skin surface. Treat each zone for 10–20 minutes per session; for combined LED and ULDM protocols, perform the LED pass first, then the ultrasound step. Repeat 1–2 times per week for 4–6 sessions as a course.

description: OMEGA PDT is a professional LED photodynamic therapy device delivering four synergistic wavelengths — 415 nm (blue), 633 nm (red), 830 nm (near-infrared), and 590 nm (yellow) — augmented by an infrared lamp for supplementary thermal stimulation. Each wavelength targets a distinct chromophore cascade: blue suppresses Cutibacterium acnes porphyrins, red stimulates fibroblast collagen synthesis, near-infrared penetrates the dermis to reduce inflammation, and yellow addresses vascular redness. The face and body configuration allows full-panel or segmented coverage for facial and décolletage protocols.
protocol: Cleanse and dry the skin, then apply the relevant photosensitiser if using ALA-PDT mode. Position the LED panel 5–10 cm above the treatment zone and select the wavelength program per the indication. Deliver 10–20 minutes per zone at the manufacturer's recommended irradiance setting. For acne PDT, pre-treat with 5-ALA and allow 30–60 minutes incubation before light exposure. Perform 4–8 sessions spaced 1–2 weeks apart.

description: DIASONO.310 is a dual-modality platform combining the Diacell Peel hydro-dermabrasion and electroporation handpiece with the Sono 310 multi-frequency ultrasonic unit operating at 3 MHz and 10 MHz. The Diacell Peel mode uses aqua peeling to mechanically exfoliate and simultaneously infuse actives, while electroporation transiently opens skin channels to drive hydrophilic molecules 200–400 μm into the epidermis. Sono 310's dual-frequency ultrasound generates thermal and mechanical effects in the dermis to support collagen remodelling and product transdermal absorption.
protocol: Begin with the Diacell aqua peel pass using sterile saline or a suitable serum at low suction pressure; perform 2–3 passes over the full face. Switch to electroporation mode and apply the active serum, delivering 3–5 passes per zone at 200–400 V with 100 μs pulse width. Complete with the Sono 310 handpiece at 3 MHz for superficial work or 10 MHz for deeper dermal stimulation, applying coupling gel and moving in slow circular strokes for 5–8 minutes per zone. Recommend 4–6 sessions at 2-week intervals.

description: HIFFECT PLUS is a multi-modal radiofrequency platform combining fractional RF microneedle, multipolar RF, and an integrated cooling system to remodel dermal collagen and improve skin elasticity. Fractional RF delivers focused thermal zones at adjustable depths (0.5–3.5 mm) to induce controlled micro-coagulation and initiate neocollagenesis, while multipolar RF heats the dermis uniformly to 40–42 °C for diffuse tightening. The integrated cooling module protects the epidermis throughout delivery, making the system suitable for fine wrinkle refinement, enlarged pore reduction, and scar remodelling.
protocol: Apply a thin layer of RF coupling gel to the treatment area. For fractional RF mode, set needle depth (1.0–2.5 mm for face, up to 3.5 mm for body), energy level, and stamp density per the planned protocol; complete 2–4 passes in a grid pattern. Switch to multipolar RF mode for the final diffuse heating pass, maintaining continuous contact and slow circular movement until target skin temperature (40–42 °C) is achieved. Perform 3–5 sessions at 4-week intervals; use sunscreen for at least 1 week post-treatment.

description: WILLCAM PLUS is a professional skin and scalp diagnosis system combining high-magnification polarised and cross-polarised imaging with dedicated analysis software to map pores, sebum distribution, melanin spots, wrinkle depth, hydration, and scalp follicular density. The smart design captures standardised images under controlled illumination conditions, generating comparative reports across sessions to guide personalised treatment planning. The scalp module applies dermoscopic-level magnification to assess follicular miniaturisation, dandruff grade, and scalp microvasculature.
protocol: Position the client in a relaxed, upright posture under consistent ambient lighting. Capture baseline images in polarised mode for surface assessment (texture, pores, spots) and in cross-polarised mode for sub-surface features (vascular patterns, deep pigmentation). Input findings into the analysis software to generate a skin score report and treatment recommendation. Re-assess using the same configuration at each follow-up to track treatment progress; schedule diagnostic sessions at 4–8 week intervals for ongoing monitoring.

description: DN64 NEEDLE is a 64-pin roller-type replaceable needle cartridge engineered for MTS (Microneedle Transdermal System) devices, available in 0.25 mm depth for epidermal channel formation and 0.5 mm depth for superficial dermal penetration. The 64-pin roller array creates a uniform matrix of micro-channels across a broad surface area in a single pass, enhancing transdermal delivery of applied actives by up to several hundred-fold compared with intact skin. CE certified; supplied 5 cartridges per box for multi-client professional use.
protocol: Cleanse and disinfect the skin thoroughly; apply a thin layer of the target serum or ampoule to the treatment zone. Insert a new cartridge into the MTS device for each client. Roll in four directions (vertical, horizontal, and two diagonal) with gentle-to-moderate pressure, completing 3–5 passes per zone. For 0.25 mm: perform 8–10 passes; for 0.5 mm: limit to 4–6 passes. Apply the remaining serum and a soothing mask post-procedure. Discard the cartridge immediately after the session.

description: DN16 NEEDLE is a 16-pin stamp-type replaceable needle cartridge for MTS devices, designed specifically for scalp protocols where roller-type cartridges are impractical due to hair density. The stamp configuration delivers precise vertical micro-channel penetration at each impression, enhancing absorption of scalp actives such as minoxidil, peptide cocktails, or growth-factor serums. CE certified; supplied 5 cartridges per box.
protocol: Part the hair to expose the target scalp zone and cleanse the area with 70% isopropyl alcohol. Apply the scalp serum or active solution to the zone. Stamp the cartridge vertically with consistent pressure in a grid pattern, spacing each impression approximately 1–2 mm apart, covering the entire planned area. Work in sections of 4 × 4 cm for even coverage. Remove excess serum with a gentle blot; do not rinse for 4 hours. Discard the cartridge in a sharps container after each session.

description: I COOL-PLUS 2ND is a six-in-one integrated skin care device combining skin scrubber ultrasonic spatula, iontophoresis, electroporation, 1 MHz ultrasonic wave, and bipolar cooling/heating in a single handset. Iontophoresis drives charged active molecules across the epidermis via a low-amperage galvanic current, while electroporation complements delivery of larger or neutral molecules by transiently disrupting lipid bilayers. The cooling mode (down to 5 °C) calms post-procedure erythema, while heating (up to 45 °C) softens sebum plugs and primes skin for deeper serum penetration.
protocol: Begin with the skin scrubber function using ultrasonic vibration and a saline or enzyme cleanser to dislodge surface debris in 2–3 slow passes. Apply the target serum and switch to iontophoresis for cationic actives or electroporation for larger molecules; treat each zone for 3–5 minutes. Follow with the 1 MHz ultrasound pass for 5–8 minutes using coupling gel. Conclude with a cooling pass at 5 °C for 2–3 minutes to reduce erythema and close channels. Recommend 4–8 sessions at 1–2 week intervals.

description: ULTRA SKIN MASTER is a five-modality aesthetic platform integrating micro-focused HIFU, skin scrubber, electroporation, automated MTS (auto-stamping), and bipolar cooling/heating into a single device. HIFU cartridges deposit thermal focal points at 1.5 mm, 3.0 mm, and 4.5 mm depths to coagulate SMAS and dermal collagen, producing lifting and tightening effects without epidermal disruption. The auto-MTS module channels the skin for subsequent electroporation-assisted serum delivery, maximising active penetration immediately post-channelling.
protocol: Start with the HIFU step: select the appropriate cartridge depth for the target tissue layer, map treatment lines on the face or neck, and deliver shots at the manufacturer's recommended energy and spacing (typically 600–800 shots for a full face). Follow with the auto-MTS pass using a 0.3–0.5 mm cartridge to create micro-channels, then immediately apply a high-potency serum and perform an electroporation pass for 3–5 minutes. Conclude with cooling at 5 °C for 2–3 minutes. HIFU sessions are typically performed once every 6–12 months; MTS and electroporation steps may be repeated monthly.

description: HYSONIC THREE WAY is a premium multi-modality aesthetic platform consolidating BLT (Bio Light Therapy), SONO (multi-frequency ultrasound), HIFU (High Intensity Focused Ultrasound), fractional RF, and integrated cooling/heating into a single clinical unit. BLT delivers targeted LED wavelengths for photobiomodulation, SONO applies 1/3/10 MHz ultrasonic energy for varying tissue penetration depths, and HIFU deposits coagulative thermal zones at defined dermis and SMAS depths for structural lifting. The cooling module prevents epidermal thermal injury during RF and HIFU delivery, enabling aggressive energy parameters with a maintained safety margin.
protocol: Sequence modalities from deepest to shallowest: begin with HIFU at the appropriate cartridge depth for the target tissue layer, then proceed to multipolar RF for broad dermal heating (target skin surface temperature 40–42 °C). Apply SONO at 1 MHz for deeper muscular relaxation or 3–10 MHz for superficial hydration enhancement. Conclude with BLT at 633 nm (red) for post-procedure recovery for 10–15 minutes, with cooling active throughout RF and HIFU steps. Full multi-modal sessions recommended every 4–8 weeks; HIFU step every 6–12 months.

description: HERA POINT is a focused HIFU device that concentrates ultrasonic energy at precise focal depths — typically 1.5 mm (papillary dermis), 3.0 mm (reticular dermis), and 4.5 mm (superficial musculoaponeurotic system) — creating discrete coagulation zones that trigger the wound-healing cascade and subsequent collagen neosynthesis. The focused thermal injury at the SMAS level replicates the tissue effect of surgical lifting without incisions, achieving progressive skin tightening and fine wrinkle reduction over 2–3 months post-treatment. CE-certified professional HIFU platform intended for face and neck lifting protocols.
protocol: Cleanse the face and apply ultrasound coupling gel. Perform a skin thickness assessment to select the appropriate cartridge depth (1.5 mm for superficial lines, 3.0 mm for dermis, 4.5 mm for SMAS and deep tissue lifting). Map the treatment grid, typically 200–800 shots for a full face and neck. Deliver shots at manufacturer-recommended fluence, maintaining 5 mm spacing between shot lines. A single full-face HIFU session is typically performed once every 6–12 months; final results are visible at 8–12 weeks post-treatment.

description: ACCUTONING is a 1450 nm diode laser specifically calibrated to target water in sebaceous glands, delivering selective photothermal energy directly to the gland without ablating the overlying epidermis. At 1450 nm, water absorption is high enough to heat sebaceous tissue to 45–50 °C, causing thermal injury to the gland and reducing sebum output — the primary driver of inflammatory acne vulgaris. Clinical studies show 40–75% reduction in inflammatory lesion count after a 3–5 session course, with minimal downtime compared with ablative resurfacing.
protocol: Apply topical anaesthetic cream 30–45 minutes pre-treatment. Remove anaesthetic and cleanse the face with a mild cleanser. Apply cooling gel and attach the 1450 nm handpiece. Select the fluence (typically 10–14 J/cm²) and pulse duration per skin phototype (Fitzpatrick I–IV). Deliver pulses in a systematic grid across the acne-prone zones; use a cryogen spray or dynamic cooling device (DCD) to protect the epidermis between pulses. Perform 3–5 sessions at 3–4 week intervals; combine with topical retinoids or antibiotics for optimal outcome.

description: HERA VACUUM is an RF vacuum device that combines high-frequency monopolar/bipolar radiofrequency with mechanical suction to synergistically heat the dermis and subcutaneous tissue while mechanically mobilising fibrous septa. The vacuum component folds the skin into the RF field, increasing the effective energy density in the target tissue and improving lymphatic drainage of liberated lipid fragments. The three interchangeable handpieces target distinct anatomical zones: a small facial handpiece for jowl and jawline, a medium body handpiece for arms and thighs, and a large body handpiece for abdomen and flank.
protocol: Apply a thin layer of RF coupling gel to the treatment area. Attach the appropriate handpiece and set vacuum suction level to patient comfort (typically 30–60 kPa), then set RF energy at the starting level recommended for the selected body zone. Move the handpiece in slow overlapping strokes, maintaining suction contact throughout, until skin surface temperature reaches 40–42 °C as confirmed by the integrated thermometer. Treat each zone for 15–20 minutes. Recommend 6–8 sessions at weekly intervals, with maintenance sessions every 4–6 weeks.

description: ULTRATONING combines multi-frequency ultrasonic therapy (1 MHz, 3 MHz, and 10 MHz) with 4 MHz radiofrequency in a single platform to address skin brightening, collagen stimulation, and subcutaneous cellulite at different tissue depths. Lower-frequency ultrasound (1 MHz) penetrates to 4–5 cm for muscle relaxation and deep lipolysis support, 3 MHz targets mid-dermal collagen remodelling, and 10 MHz works in the superficial epidermis and papillary dermis for brightening and surface smoothing. The 4 MHz RF channel delivers controlled resistive heating to fibroblasts to upregulate collagen and elastin synthesis.
protocol: Apply ultrasound coupling gel to the treatment area and select the operating frequency based on target tissue depth. Use 1 MHz for body zones requiring deep tissue heating or cellulite treatment; use 3 MHz for facial and décolletage rejuvenation; use 10 MHz for superficial brightening and pore refinement. Transition to 4 MHz RF for dermal heating, maintaining slow circular or linear strokes until target tissue temperature is achieved (40–42 °C). Perform 6–8 sessions at 1–2 week intervals for body programmes; facial programmes typically require 4–6 sessions monthly.

description: AUTO-DN MTS PEN is a professional electric microneedling pen delivering precise vertical needle penetration at adjustable depths from 0.1 mm to 2.0 mm, with three speed settings ranging from 5,000 to 7,000 RPM. The vertical stamping action — unlike manual dermarollers — minimises lateral tearing of the epidermis and creates uniform micro-channels for consistent transdermal delivery of applied actives. The ampoule-compatible tip allows direct serum connection for simultaneous channelling and infusion within a single pass.
protocol: Cleanse and optionally anaesthetise the treatment area (topical cream 20–30 min for depths above 1.0 mm). Select the needle depth appropriate to the indication: 0.25–0.5 mm for mesotherapy serum delivery, 0.5–1.0 mm for collagen induction, 1.0–2.0 mm for scar or stretch mark protocols. Set speed to the lowest setting (5,000 RPM) for sensitive areas and the highest (7,000 RPM) for thicker skin. Move the pen in overlapping cross-hatch passes until mild pinpoint bleeding is achieved at collagen-induction depths. Apply recovery serum and discard the needle cartridge immediately after.

description: AUTO-DN SMART is a professional cordless electric microneedling pen offering six speed levels and a depth range of 0.1–3.0 mm, enabling precise titration from superficial epidermal serum delivery to deep dermal collagen induction and body scar remodelling. The wireless design eliminates cable drag, giving the operator full positional freedom during treatment, including scalp and body protocols. The rechargeable battery delivers consistent needle velocity throughout a full treatment session without power drop-off.
protocol: Fully charge the device before each session. Apply topical anaesthetic for depths exceeding 1.0 mm (30–45 minutes contact time). Select the depth per the protocol: 0.25–0.5 mm for superficial active delivery (no anaesthesia required), 0.5–1.5 mm for facial collagen induction, 1.5–3.0 mm for body scars, stretch marks, or scalp. Set speed (levels 1–2 for sensitive areas, 4–6 for thick skin zones). Perform overlapping grid passes, applying serum to the surface throughout. Use a disposable cartridge per client and discard after the session.

description: The 12 PIN and 42 PIN needle cartridges are single-use replacement tips engineered for the AUTO MTS PEN platform, covering penetration depths up to 2.0 mm at a 0.3G pin gauge. The 12-pin configuration concentrates needle density for targeted spot treatments such as acne scars, deep expression lines, and focal pigmentation, while the 42-pin format provides broader surface coverage for full-face collagen induction or large body areas. Both cartridges accept direct ampoule attachment for simultaneous needle-channel delivery.
protocol: Attach the cartridge securely to the AUTO MTS PEN and confirm the depth stop is set per the planned protocol (0.25–0.5 mm for mesotherapy delivery, 0.5–1.0 mm for collagen induction, up to 2.0 mm for scar treatment). Apply the active serum to the skin surface. Use the 12-pin cartridge for focal high-density treatment or the 42-pin cartridge for broad-coverage passes. Complete 2–4 passes per zone in a cross-hatch pattern. Discard the cartridge in a sharps container after each client session; cartridges are non-resterilisable.

description: NANO NEEDLE cartridges are shallow-penetration nano-tip arrays available in 5 × 5 (100 points) and 8 × 8 (188 points) configurations for the AUTO MTS PEN platform. Operating at sub-dermal depths, the nano-tip format creates transient nano-scale channels in the stratum corneum without reaching the viable epidermis, enabling substantially enhanced penetration of cosmeceutical serums, peptides, and growth factors without erythema or downtime. The 5 × 5 array is suited for periorbital and lip border work, while the 8 × 8 array covers broader facial zones in fewer passes.
protocol: Cleanse and prepare the skin; no anaesthetic is required for nano-depth protocols. Attach the nano cartridge to the AUTO MTS PEN and select the shallowest depth setting (0.1–0.25 mm). Apply a thin layer of the target active (peptide solution, HA serum, or growth-factor ampoule) to the skin surface. Move the pen in slow, overlapping passes, completing 4–6 passes per zone in alternating directions. Apply additional serum after the final pass and follow with a cooling mask. Discard the cartridge after each session; recommended 4–6 sessions at 1–2 week intervals.

description: JOLLA Cleansing Lotion is a large-format salon-grade facial cleanser formulated with emollient surfactants and humectant actives to remove make-up, sebum, and environmental particulates while preserving the skin's natural moisture barrier. The gentle surfactant system minimises transepidermal water loss during the cleansing step, making it suitable as a first or double-cleanse product for all skin types including sensitive and post-procedure skin. The 1000 mL format is optimised for high-throughput salon and clinic environments.
protocol: Dispense 2–3 mL onto the fingertips or a soft facial sponge. Apply to damp skin using circular massage strokes for 30–60 seconds, working from the centre of the face outward. Emulsify with water and rinse thoroughly, or remove with damp cotton pads for a non-rinse cabin application. Follow immediately with toner and the planned active serum step. For post-procedure skin (post-laser, post-peel, post-needling), apply with minimal friction and pat dry rather than rubbing.

description: JOLLA Green Tea Modeling Mask is a salon-grade alginate peel-off mask enriched with green tea catechins (EGCG) to deliver antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and skin-brightening benefits during the mask-step within a professional facial protocol. Alginate, derived from brown algae, forms a firm elastic gel film on setting that locks in moisture and active ingredients while providing a mild cooling compression effect to reduce erythema and support barrier recovery. The 1000 g powder format is formulated for mixing in a professional bowl with cold water.
protocol: Measure 40–50 g of powder and mix with 100–120 mL of cold water in a mask bowl, stirring vigorously for 30–60 seconds until a smooth, lump-free gel is achieved. Apply the gel evenly across the face and décolletage using a mask brush in a single even layer approximately 5 mm thick. Leave to set for 15–20 minutes until firm. Remove by lifting from the chin edge and peeling upward in one continuous movement. Rinse residue, then apply the final serum and moisturiser. Suitable for weekly in-salon use.

description: JOLLA Peppermint Modeling Mask is a salon-grade alginate peel-off mask formulated with peppermint extract (menthol and menthone) to deliver an immediate cooling and vasoconstrictive sensation that soothes post-procedure redness and reduces the perception of sensitivity. The alginate matrix forms a firm occlusive film that enhances penetration of the peppermint actives while providing physical compression to calm reactive skin. The cooling profile makes it particularly useful after laser treatments, chemical peels, and microneedling sessions.
protocol: Measure 40–50 g of powder and mix with 100–120 mL of cold water in a mask bowl, stirring for 30–60 seconds until smooth. For a more intense cooling effect, use chilled water (4–8 °C). Apply an even 5 mm-thick layer across the face using a mask brush, avoiding the orbital rim and lips. Allow to set for 15–20 minutes. Remove by lifting from the chin and peeling upward. Rinse residue and apply a soothing serum and barrier repair cream. Use immediately post-procedure or as a weekly calming treatment.

description: MisAdi Lipo is a lipolytic mesotherapy solution formulated with phosphatidylcholine (PPC) and sodium deoxycholate (SDC) for non-surgical fat reduction on both facial and body areas. PPC disrupts adipocyte phospholipid bilayers, promoting lipolysis and emulsification of triglycerides, while SDC acts as a surfactant bile salt that solubilises the released lipid droplets for lymphatic clearance. The 8 mL vial format supports dosing flexibility for small facial zones (double chin, jowls, buccal fat) as well as larger body compartments.
protocol: Using a 27–30G needle, inject 0.2 mL per depot subcutaneously into the fat compartment at 6–13 mm depth, spacing injections 1 cm apart across the planned treatment grid. For the submental or jowl zone, administer 1–3 mL per session depending on the volume of fat present. For body areas (arms, abdomen, flanks), administer up to 6–8 mL per zone per session. Perform 3–6 sessions at 4-week intervals; expect peak effect at 4–6 weeks post-final session.

description: MisAdi Lipo (Face and Body) single-vial format contains the same PPC and sodium deoxycholate lipolytic composition as the 4-vial pack, supplied as a single 8 mL sterile vial for practitioners treating one zone per session or trialling the product. PPC at concentrations of 50 mg/mL disrupts adipocyte membranes through detergent action, while SDC at 25 mg/mL ensures solubilisation and lymphatic clearance of the released lipid content. The single-vial size is optimised for facial protocols such as submental fat and buccal fat reduction.
protocol: Inject 0.2 mL per depot subcutaneously using a 27–30G needle at 6–13 mm depth, with injection points spaced 1 cm apart. For the submental zone, administer 1–2 mL per session; for the buccal fat area, 0.5–1.5 mL per session. Limit total volume per session to 8 mL across all treated facial zones. Perform up to 3 sessions at 4-week intervals for the face. Expect moderate swelling for 48–72 hours post-injection; clinical results are apparent at 4–8 weeks post-session.

description: Barbie Slim is a non-surgical lipolytic injection solution combining plant-derived phosphatidylcholine (PPC) with deoxycholic acid and botanical extracts formulated for face and body fat-contouring applications. PPC from soya lecithin emulsifies triglycerides within adipocytes, while deoxycholic acid solubilises the released lipids for phagocytic clearance via the lymphatic system. Botanical co-actives in the formulation support lymphatic drainage and reduce post-injection oedema, shortening recovery time compared with formulations containing deoxycholate alone.
protocol: Using a 27–30G needle, inject 0.2 mL per depot subcutaneously at 6–13 mm depth, with deposits 1 cm apart across the treatment zone. For facial contouring (jawline, double chin), administer 2–4 mL per session; for body areas, administer 4–10 mL per zone per session, up to 20 mL total per session. Perform 3–6 sessions at 3–4 week intervals. Monitor for post-injection swelling, which typically peaks at 48–72 hours; advise lymphatic massage 48 hours post-procedure to support drainage.

description: DR.LIPO+ is a next-generation lipolytic solution formulated with phosphatidylcholine (PPC), sodium deoxycholate (SDC), L-carnitine, and collagen-stimulating peptides (including Acetyl Hexapeptide-8 and Palmitoyl Pentapeptide-4) for body fat dissolution with concurrent collagen support. PPC and SDC work synergistically to lyse adipocyte membranes and solubilise released lipids, while L-carnitine facilitates mitochondrial fatty-acid beta-oxidation to accelerate metabolic clearance of liberated triglycerides. The peptide complex initiates fibroblast collagen production in the treated zone, helping prevent skin laxity following fat volume reduction.
protocol: Using a 27–30G needle, inject 0.2–0.5 mL per depot subcutaneously at 6–13 mm depth, spacing deposits 1 cm apart across the planned grid. For larger body zones (abdomen, flanks, thighs), administer 5–10 mL per zone per session, up to 50 mL total. For smaller areas (arms, knee pads), administer 2–5 mL per zone. Perform 4–6 sessions at 3–4 week intervals. Advise the patient to maintain adequate hydration and mild exercise between sessions to support lymphatic clearance.

description: DR.LIPO+V is the smaller-volume variant of the DR.LIPO+ PPC-SDC lipolytic cocktail, supplied in 5 mL vials for precision facial and targeted body applications. The formulation retains the core PPC, sodium deoxycholate, L-carnitine, and peptide complex of the full-size product but is presented in a volume optimised for facial zones including the submental region, buccal fat compartment, and jowl area, where total per-session volumes rarely exceed 3–5 mL per zone. The smaller vial format also supports multi-zone body work where the practitioner requires precise individual-zone dosing with minimised wastage.
protocol: Inject 0.2 mL per depot subcutaneously at 6–13 mm depth using a 27–30G needle, spacing deposits 1 cm apart. For the submental area, administer 1–3 mL per session; for the buccal fat region, 0.5–2 mL per session. For body applications, use up to 5 mL per small zone (inner knee, arm focal deposits). Perform up to 6 sessions at 3–4 week intervals; allow 4–6 weeks between the final session and clinical assessment of results.

description: LIPOLAB GMS is a professional PPC-based lipolytic cocktail formulated with phosphatidylcholine and sodium deoxycholate, presented in a 10-vial pack for high-volume body contour protocols. The GMS designation indicates a grade optimised for larger subcutaneous fat compartments such as the abdomen, flanks, inner thighs, and upper arms, where higher total injection volumes per session are required. The 10-vial configuration supports multi-zone treatment within a single clinical session without the need for multiple product purchases.
protocol: Using a 27–30G needle, inject 0.2 mL per depot subcutaneously at 6–13 mm depth, with deposits 1 cm apart across the planned grid. Administer 5–10 mL per zone per session for larger body areas; do not exceed 50 mL total per session across all zones. Perform 4–6 sessions at 3–4 week intervals. Advise patients to expect swelling and firmness in the treated area for 3–7 days post-injection; results typically become visible at 4–8 weeks after each session.

description: LIPOLAB V LINE MEDIHUE is a specialised PPC-based lipolytic cocktail containing phosphatidylcholine (50 mg/mL), sodium deoxycholate (25 mg/mL), and 2% lidocaine, formulated specifically for V-line facial contouring procedures. The lidocaine component provides immediate local anaesthesia at the injection site, substantially reducing procedure discomfort without the need for separate topical or injectable anaesthesia. The V-line formulation is pH-adjusted for facial tissue compatibility, reducing the post-injection inflammatory response compared with non-optimised PPC/SDC solutions.
protocol: Using a 27–30G, 13 mm needle, inject 0.2 mL per depot subcutaneously at 6–10 mm depth along the jawline, jowl, and submental zone, spacing deposits 1 cm apart. Administer 2–5 mL per session for standard jawline contouring; up to 8 mL if submental fat is also included in the same session. Perform 3–5 sessions at 3–4 week intervals. Advise the patient that swelling typically peaks at 24–48 hours and resolves within 3–5 days; results become apparent progressively from week 4 post-final session.

description: SUPER V-LINE SOL GMS is a GMS-grade PPC-based lipolytic cocktail formulated at higher phosphatidylcholine concentration for enhanced fat cell lysis in the facial V-line zone. The formulation targets submental fat, jowl deposits, and lower-face fullness by disrupting adipocyte phospholipid bilayers, releasing triglyceride content for lymphatic elimination. The GMS-grade concentration delivers a more pronounced fat-dissolving effect per millilitre, allowing practitioners to achieve visible contour improvement with fewer sessions and lower total injected volumes.
protocol: Inject 0.2 mL per depot subcutaneously using a 27–30G needle at 6–10 mm depth, with depots spaced 1 cm apart along the planned V-line grid (chin, submental, jawline, and jowl zones). Administer 2–4 mL per zone per session; do not exceed 10 mL total across the face per session. Perform 3–5 sessions at 3–4 week intervals. Assess clinical response at 6 weeks after the final session; advise gentle lymphatic massage from day 3 post-procedure to support fat clearance.

description: Yellow Bottle is a multi-active lipolytic mesotherapy cocktail formulated for combined face and body fat-contouring applications. The proprietary blend contains phosphatidylcholine, sodium deoxycholate, L-carnitine, and caffeine — a xanthine alkaloid that inhibits phosphodiesterase, elevating intracellular cyclic AMP and stimulating hormone-sensitive lipase activity within adipocytes for enhanced lipolysis. Caffeine also promotes local microcirculation and lymphatic drainage, reducing post-procedure oedema and accelerating clearance of the released lipid metabolites.
protocol: Using a 27–30G needle, inject 0.2 mL per depot subcutaneously at 6–13 mm depth, with deposits 1 cm apart. For facial zones (jawline, double chin, buccal), administer 2–4 mL per session; for body zones (abdomen, flanks, thighs), administer 5–10 mL per zone, up to 50 mL total per session. Perform 4–6 sessions at 3–4 week intervals. Advise patients to increase water intake and perform light aerobic exercise 24–48 hours post-procedure to support lymphatic mobilisation of fatty acids.

description: Pine Bottle is an advanced lipolytic solution formulated with a proprietary blend of phosphatidylcholine, sodium deoxycholate, and pine-derived botanical extracts that contribute antioxidant and anti-inflammatory co-active properties to the fat-dissolving base. The pine extract component contains proanthocyanidins that scavenge free radicals generated during adipocyte lysis, reducing oxidative-stress-mediated tissue damage in the treated compartment. The formulation is designed for subcutaneous fat zones where post-injection inflammation and skin texture changes are of concern, such as the perioral region, neck, and visible body areas.
protocol: Using a 27–30G needle, inject 0.2 mL per depot subcutaneously at 6–13 mm depth, spacing deposits 1 cm apart across the treatment grid. For small facial areas, administer 1–3 mL per session; for body zones, administer 5–10 mL per zone, up to 50 mL total per session. Perform 4–6 sessions at 3–4 week intervals. Post-procedure redness and swelling should resolve within 72 hours; pine antioxidant components may help reduce post-procedure bruising compared with standard PPC/SDC formulations.

description: Ultra Light is a PPC-based lipolytic cocktail formulated in small 2 cc ampoules for precise, low-volume facial fat-dissolving protocols targeting the jawline, cheeks, and submental area. The concentrated phosphatidylcholine formulation achieves maximum lipolytic effect per millilitre, allowing practitioners to treat delicate facial compartments such as buccal fat and jowl deposits with carefully controlled micro-deposits that minimise diffusion into adjacent structures. The rapid-penetration formula reduces time to clinical onset of lipolysis.
protocol: Using a 27–30G needle, inject 0.1–0.2 mL per depot subcutaneously at 6–10 mm depth, with deposits 1 cm apart. For the buccal fat region, administer 0.5–1.5 mL per side per session; for the jawline and jowl, 1–2 mL per side. The total facial volume per session should not exceed 5–6 mL. Perform 3–5 sessions at 3–4 week intervals. Facial zones require precise anatomical targeting to avoid inadvertent diffusion into the parotid or masseter; mark injection points before starting.

description: F&B (Face and Body) is a PPC-based lipolytic fat-burning cocktail formulated in 2 cc vials for dual facial and body contouring applications. The active blend of phosphatidylcholine and sodium deoxycholate is calibrated at a concentration suitable for both the delicate subcutaneous fat compartments of the face and the thicker fat layers of the body, offering practitioners a versatile single-product protocol without switching formulations between areas. The small vial format enables accurate micro-dosing across multiple treatment zones within a single session.
protocol: For facial zones: inject 0.1–0.2 mL per depot at 6–10 mm depth using a 27–30G needle, administer 1–3 mL per facial zone per session. For body zones: inject 0.2–0.5 mL per depot at 6–13 mm depth, administer 4–8 mL per body zone per session, up to 20 mL total per session. Perform 3–6 sessions at 3–4 week intervals. Assess results 6 weeks after the final session; advise patients to maintain hydration and avoid high-fat meals on treatment days.

description: Dr. Lipo Prime is a PPC-based lipolytic cocktail formulated for professional mesotherapy fat-dissolving procedures on both face and body. The composition combines phosphatidylcholine, sodium deoxycholate, L-carnitine, and growth-factor peptides to achieve adipocyte lysis while simultaneously promoting skin tightening in the treated area — an important consideration for larger body fat zones where significant volume reduction can cause secondary skin laxity. The 2 mL vials enable accurate dose calculation for multi-zone body sessions.
protocol: Using a 27–30G needle, inject 0.2 mL per depot subcutaneously at 6–13 mm depth, spacing deposits 1 cm apart. For facial applications, administer 1–3 mL per zone per session; for body applications, administer 5–10 mL per zone per session, up to 40 mL total across all body zones. Perform 4–6 sessions at 3–4 week intervals. Peptide co-actives in the formulation support neocollagenesis between sessions, helping maintain skin firmness as fat volume reduces.

description: Kabelline is a phosphatidylcholine and deoxycholic acid lipolytic solution formulated for subcutaneous fat-dissolving treatments on body contouring areas. Deoxycholic acid acts as a bile salt surfactant that penetrates adipocyte membranes and disrupts cell wall integrity, triggering cell lysis and subsequent inflammatory phagocytic removal of fat cell debris by macrophages. Each 8 mL vial supports treatment of one to two adjacent body zones per session, and the 5-vial pack provides sufficient product for a complete treatment course.
protocol: Using a 27–30G needle, inject 0.2 mL per depot subcutaneously at 6–13 mm depth, spacing deposits 1 cm apart across the treatment grid. Administer 4–10 mL per zone per session, with a total per-session volume not exceeding 40–50 mL. Perform up to 6 sessions at minimum 1-week intervals, with an optimal schedule of 3–4 week intervals. Expect swelling, redness, and firmness in the treated area for 3–7 days post-injection; these are expected signs of the inflammatory lipolysis cascade. Assess final outcomes at 8 weeks after the last session.

description: VNS is a mesotherapy lipolytic cocktail combining phosphatidylcholine, sodium deoxycholate, and supporting co-actives designed for professional body-contouring protocols targeting localised subcutaneous fat deposits. The formulation's PPC concentration is calibrated to achieve progressive lipolysis with each session, with clinical fat-layer reduction becoming measurable by ultrasound or caliper at 4–6 weeks post-injection. The 10 mL vial format provides sufficient volume for full treatment of moderately sized body zones such as the inner thigh, knee deposit, or upper arm in a single session.
protocol: Using a 27–30G needle, inject 0.2 mL per depot subcutaneously at 6–13 mm depth, with deposits 1 cm apart. Administer 5–10 mL per zone per session. Map the treatment area with a skin marker to ensure even depot distribution. Perform 4–6 sessions at 3–4 week intervals. Monitor for expected post-procedure erythema and oedema (resolving within 48–72 hours); photograph the zone at baseline and before each session to document progressive fat reduction.

description: Slim Point Face and Body is a fat-dissolving lipolysis ampoule formulated with a concentrated PPC and deoxycholate base for professional contouring across both facial and large body fat compartments. The 30 mL ampoule format provides sufficient volume for high-volume body treatments (abdomen, flanks, thighs) or multiple small facial zones within a single session, reducing per-session product costs compared with smaller vial formats. The pH-balanced formulation is designed to minimise injection pain and tissue irritation relative to standard-concentration deoxycholate products.
protocol: Using a 27–30G needle, inject 0.2–0.5 mL per depot subcutaneously at 6–13 mm depth, spacing deposits 1 cm apart. For facial contouring, administer 2–5 mL per zone per session; for body contouring, administer 10–25 mL per large zone (abdomen, flanks), up to 50–60 mL total per session. Perform 3–6 sessions at 3–4 week intervals. Advise patients to perform lymphatic self-massage 48 hours post-procedure to support fat clearance.

description: Lipo Shrinker is a lipolytic mesotherapy cocktail formulated for subcutaneous fat reduction in localised body deposits. The active blend of phosphatidylcholine and sodium deoxycholate is presented in a 5.5 mL single-use vial, providing a controlled unit dose for targeted treatment of small to medium fat zones such as the inner knee, bra-line, or upper arm deposits. The compact vial volume encourages precise zone-specific dosing and reduces the risk of inadvertent over-treatment of small anatomical areas.
protocol: Using a 27–30G needle, inject 0.2 mL per depot subcutaneously at 6–13 mm depth, spacing deposits 1 cm apart. Administer 3–5.5 mL per small zone per session. For larger zones, use multiple vials as needed to cover the full treatment grid. Perform 4–6 sessions at 3–4 week intervals, assessing clinical fat-volume response before each session. Advise patients to avoid anti-inflammatory medications (NSAIDs) for 48 hours post-injection, as mild inflammatory response is part of the lipolysis mechanism.

description: Botulax 100U is a lyophilised purified botulinum toxin type A complex manufactured by Hugel Inc. (South Korea) from the Clostridium botulinum strain CBFC26, formulated with 0.5 mg human serum albumin and 0.9 mg sodium chloride per 100-unit vial. The toxin blocks acetylcholine release at the neuromuscular junction by cleaving SNAP-25, producing reversible chemodenervation of the targeted muscle and temporary reduction of dynamic expression lines including glabellar furrows, forehead lines, and crow's feet. Each 100-unit vial is suitable for a standard full-face aesthetic treatment covering multiple sites.
protocol: Reconstitute with 1.0–2.5 mL of preservative-free 0.9% sodium chloride to achieve a working concentration of 4–10 U per 0.1 mL. Using a 30–32G, 13 mm needle, inject 2–5 U per point intramuscularly at standard anatomical landmarks. For the glabellar complex, administer 20–30 U total across 5 injection points; for forehead lines, 10–20 U across 4–6 points; for crow's feet, 6–15 U per side across 3–4 points. Onset is 3–7 days; peak effect at 2 weeks; duration 3–6 months. Use reconstituted product within 24 hours, stored at 2–8 °C.

description: Botulax 200U is a higher-unit vial of the Hugel Inc. purified botulinum toxin type A complex (strain CBFC26), formulated identically to the 100U product but with twice the unit content per vial. The larger vial is suited to high-volume treatment sessions covering multiple facial zones or combining facial aesthetics with functional indications such as masseter reduction (bruxism, square jaw), hyperhidrosis (axillary or palmar), and neck platysmal band correction, where total session dose routinely exceeds 100 units.
protocol: Reconstitute with 2.0–5.0 mL of preservative-free 0.9% saline to achieve a working concentration of 4–10 U per 0.1 mL. For masseter reduction, inject 20–30 U per masseter into the bulk of the muscle using a 30G needle, with 3–5 injection points per side. For axillary hyperhidrosis, inject 50 U per axilla across 10–15 grid points (5 U/point), after minor starch-iodine test mapping. For facial aesthetics, apply standard dosing across glabella, forehead, and crow's feet as per 100U protocol. Duration 3–6 months; use within 24 hours of reconstitution.

description: Botulax 300U is the highest unit-count vial in the Botulax range, manufactured by Hugel Inc. from purified botulinum toxin type A (strain CBFC26), and intended for practitioners performing high-volume combined aesthetic and therapeutic treatment sessions within a single appointment. The 300U vial eliminates the need for multiple vial reconstitutions during lengthy sessions and reduces per-unit cost for established high-volume aesthetic clinics. The biological composition — human serum albumin, sodium chloride excipients — is identical to the 100U and 200U variants.
protocol: Reconstitute with 3.0–7.5 mL of preservative-free 0.9% saline to achieve the desired working concentration. For combined full-face aesthetic treatment plus masseter and trapezius or neck bands in a single session, total doses may reach 200–300 units; the 300U vial accommodates this without mid-session reconstitution. Apply standard anatomical dosing across all target muscle groups using a 30–32G needle at intramuscular depth. Discard any unused reconstituted product at the end of the session; do not store for reuse beyond 24 hours post-reconstitution.

description: Metox 100U is a Korean purified botulinum toxin type A preparation manufactured by Maypharm Co., Ltd., presented as a white lyophilised powder containing 100 units of neurotoxin with human serum albumin and sodium chloride excipients per vial. The toxin cleaves SNAP-25 at the presynaptic terminal, blocking calcium-triggered acetylcholine vesicle fusion and producing reversible muscle chemodenervation with clinical onset at 3–7 days and duration of 6–9 months. Metox is used for cosmetic reduction of dynamic wrinkles (glabellar lines, forehead, crow's feet) and functional applications including hyperhidrosis and blepharospasm.
protocol: Reconstitute with 2.5 mL of preservative-free 0.9% sodium chloride to achieve a concentration of 4 U per 0.1 mL. Using a 30–32G needle, inject intramuscularly at standard anatomical landmarks: glabella 20–30 U across 5 points, forehead 10–20 U across 4–6 points, periocular crow's feet 6–12 U per side across 3 points. Allow 14 days for full effect assessment before considering touch-up dosing. Duration of effect is 6–9 months; schedule repeat treatments accordingly. Store the lyophilised vial at 2–8 °C and use reconstituted product within 24 hours.

description: Metox 200U is the higher-unit variant of the Maypharm Co. botulinum toxin type A product, providing 200 units of purified neurotoxin per vial for extended treatment sessions combining multiple facial muscle groups or for therapeutic indications requiring higher total dosing. The formulation composition — lyophilised neurotoxin with human serum albumin and sodium chloride — is identical to the 100U variant; the 200U vial simply offers increased content to minimise mid-session reconstitution. Clinical onset, efficacy, and duration of effect are equivalent to the 100U product on a unit-for-unit basis.
protocol: Reconstitute with 5.0 mL of preservative-free 0.9% sodium chloride to achieve a concentration of 4 U per 0.1 mL, or adjust diluent volume to match preferred working dilution. For combined facial treatment including glabella, forehead, crow's feet, and lip area in a single session, total dosing typically requires 80–150 U; the 200U vial accommodates full-session use. For masseter reduction in combination with facial aesthetics, add 25–50 U per masseter to the session total. Use within 24 hours of reconstitution; store the lyophilised vial at 2–8 °C before use.
